name: Australian Oilseeds Holdings Ltd
business: The company manufactures and sells chemical-free, non-GMO, sustainable edible oils and products derived from oilseeds, including cold-pressed vegetable oils and vegetable protein meals. For fiscal years 2024 and 2023, approximately 85% and 89% of total revenue came from the sale of cold-pressed vegetable oils, with the balance from vegetable protein meals. The company sells to wholesale distributors, supermarket chains, and export markets in Australia, New Zealand, Japan, and the United States under the trademark Good Earth Oils.
sellsTo: export markets in Australia, New Zealand, Japan, and the United States

exposures.sentence: The Company is focused on the manufacture and sale of chemical free, non-GMO, sustainable edible oils and products derived from oilseeds.

This facility will produce edible oil feedstocks to meet the growing Asia-Pacific market, and bio-diesel feedstock to fuel the renewable energy revolution, to eventually reach a total oilseed crushing capacity of 200 tons per day along with capabilities to bleach and deodorize the oilseeds at a capacity of 50 tons per day.
The Company produces organic food-grade oils and vegetable protein meals by means of cold pressing extraction from chemical and GMO-free oilseeds.

The Company s farmers employ regenerative farming practices such as conservative tillage and minimal use of chemicals and fertilizer to grow produce with no residue and increase carbon sequestration, thereby pulling more carbon from the atmosphere and sequestering higher carbon amounts in the soil.

catalysts.event: Expansion of Cootamundra facility completed and full operation commenced
catalysts.sentence: The current capacity of the Company s primary processing site in Cootamundra, NSW was expanded in 2024 with expansion completed with full operation in the expanded Cootamundra facility in August 2024.
The Company will renegotiate the renewal of the contract with Costco in November 2024, which has not yet commenced, given the current strong demand for Good Earth Oils products in Costco stores.

The groundbreaking will occur in the first quarter of 2025 calendar year with the construction period expected from January 2025 to December 2026 and the receipt of the certificate of completion is expected in March 2025.

The groundbreaking will occur in the first quarter of 2025 calendar year with the construction period expected from January 2025 to December 2026 and the receipt of the certificate of completion is expected in March 2025.

tamClaims.claim: Global oilseed market size was USD 249.05 billion in 2023, projected to be USD 373.32 billion by 2033, CAGR 4.13%.
tamClaims.sentence: According to the 2024 study by Precedence Research, the global oilseed market size was valued at USD 249.05 billion in 2023, and it is projected to be worth around USD 373.32 billion by 2033 making a CAGR of 4.13% during the forecast period 2024 to 2033.

tamClaims.claim: Asia Pacific oilseed market size was USD 87.46 billion in 2023, expected to reach USD 140.38 billion by 2033, CAGR 37.7%.
tamClaims.sentence: According to the 2024 study by Precedence Research, the Asia Pacific oilseed market size, which includes Australian, was valued at USD$87.46 billion in 2023 and is expected to hit around USD$140.38 billion by 2033, growing at a CAGR of 37.7% from 2024 to 2033.

tamClaims.claim: Global non-GMO food market size was USD 740.65 billion in 2023, projected to grow to USD 2,003.68 billion by 2032, CAGR 11.94%.
tamClaims.sentence: The global non-GMO food market size was USD$740.65 billion in 2023 and is projected to grow from USD$895.36 billion in 2024 to USD$2,003.68 billion by 2032, growing at a CAGR of 11.94% during the 2024-2032 period according to a study released August 14, 2024, by Fortune Business Insights.
